pi_items.source: Inspected shapefile directly with Python shapefile library
Natural Earth 110m data analysis: 17 cities >= 10M, 21 cities >= 5M and < 10M

Natural Earth 110m data includes Tokyo (35.7M), New York (19.0M), Mexico City (19.0M), Mumbai (18.9M), São Paulo (18.8M), Shanghai (14.9M), Kolkata (14.7M), Dhaka (12.8M), Buenos Aires (12.7M), Los Angeles (12.5M), Cairo (11.9M), Rio de Janeiro (11.7M), Ōsaka (11.3M), Beijing (11.1M), Manila (11.1M), Moscow (10.4M), Istanbul (10.1M)
setup_geoserver.sh lines 120, 186 publish ne_110m_populated_places as ne_populated_places in ne workspace
Natural Earth 110m shapefile contains field named POP_MAX (uppercase); case handling depends on PostGIS import and GeoServer configuration

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real Natural Earth 110m populated places data (243 total cities, dated 2022-05-13) with a POP_MAX attribute for filtering. GeoServer 2.25.2 is deployed with admin credentials admin/Admin123!. The dataset contains 17 cities with populations >= 10 million and 38 cities >= 5 million. The test value in metadata is 5,000,000 (matching export_result.sh), but the task description contradictorily states 10,000,000 as the test threshold. The layer is published as ne:ne_populated_places. The expected stored query name is 'GetLargeCities' with parameter 'minPop'.
